name: sim_integration_xgi_multishell_coexistence

pytorch.reason: not used — multi-shell coexistence is modeled as hyperedge structure; tensor operations not needed for this probe

pyg.reason: not used — PyG graph message passing couples a different shell-coupling lego; XGI is the primary graph object here

z3.reason: not used — coexistence admissibility is verified structurally via hyperedge size and deduplication, not z3 boolean constraints

cvc5.reason: not used — formal proof layer not required for hyperedge size verification; structural checks suffice

sympy.reason: not used — symbolic algebra not needed; coexistence is a combinatorial counting problem on hyperedges

clifford.reason: not used — geometric algebra couples a different geometry lego; multi-shell coexistence is a topology/graph problem

geomstats.reason: not used — Riemannian geometry not required; coexistence states are discrete hyperedges, not manifold points

e3nn.reason: not used — equivariant networks couple a different symmetry lego; hyperedge coexistence does not require SE(3) equivariance

rustworkx.reason: rustworkx converts the XGI line graph to a rustworkx graph for shortest-path between coexistence states; compatibility = path length

xgi.reason: Hypergraph, edges.members, nodes.memberships, convert.to_line_graph are all primary; multi-shell coexistence states represented directly as hyperedges

toponetx.reason: not used — cell complexes couple a different combinatorial topology lego; XGI hyperedges are the appropriate primitive here

gudhi.reason: not used — persistent homology couples a different filtration lego; not needed for discrete hyperedge coexistence counting

p1_5shell_4coexistence_states.note: 5 shells, 4 coexistence states; hyperedge sizes match co-existing shell count; node degrees match membership counts; line graph has 4 nodes; rustworkx shortest paths computed — coexistence states survived structural verification

n1_contradictory_self_coexistence.note: shell 0 appears twice in hyperedge [0,0,1]; after deduplication actual size < stated cardinality; self-coexistence excluded by structural constraint — size reduced from 3 to 2

b1_all_shells_hyperedge.note: all-shells hyperedge size = num_shells = 5; boundary coexistence state survived with correct cardinality

b2_empty_hypergraph.note: empty hypergraph has 0 edges; trivial boundary case survived as structurally valid

b3_single_node_rustworkx.note: single coexistence state; rustworkx line graph has 1 node, 0 edges; isolated coexistence state survived

summary: XGI hyperedges directly represent multi-shell coexistence states. Hyperedge sizes match co-existing shell counts. Node degrees match membership counts. Line graph nodes = coexistence state count. Rustworkx shortest paths reveal coexistence state compatibility. Self-coexistence excluded by deduplication constraint. XGI + rustworkx are load-bearing.
